rules.statement: Every public function and class MUST be preceded by a '# ID:' anchor comment on the line immediately above the def/class keyword. The anchor gives each public symbol a stable identity that survives renames and refactors. Private symbols (names starting with an underscore) are exempt.
rules.rationale: Stable identity is the foundation everything else builds on: traceability, cross-references, and consequence logging all key off the symbol ID rather than its name or line number. This is the rule the demo showcases because it is unambiguous and trivially fixable.

rules.statement: Public functions and classes MUST have a docstring that states their intent. A docstring is the contract a future reader — human or tool — relies on.
Importable library code MUST NOT use print(). Use a logger so output is structured, leveled, and routable. print() in shipped code is almost always a debugging leftover.
Code MUST NOT swallow exceptions with a bare 'except:' or 'except Exception: pass'. Name the exception you expect and handle it; a silent except hides the bug you most need to see.
